questions.question: What is the correct promotion hierarchy in Java, from smallest to largest?
options: byte -> short -> int -> long -> float -> double
byte -> short -> int -> float -> long -> double
short -> byte -> int -> long -> float -> double
byte -> char -> int -> long -> float -> double
questions.answer: byte -> short -> int -> long -> float -> double

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a widening conversion?
options: Assigning a double to an int
Assigning an int to a double
Assigning a long to a byte
Assigning a float to an int
questions.answer: Assigning an int to a double

questions.question: What is the result of casting (int) 9.99 in Java?
Why does Java require an explicit cast for narrowing conversions?
options: Because narrowing conversions are always faster
Because data loss may occur when converting to a smaller type
Because Java does not support narrowing conversions
Because the JVM cannot perform narrowing automatically
questions.answer: Because data loss may occur when converting to a smaller type

questions.question: What happens when you cast int 130 to a byte?
options: The value wraps around due to overflow, producing -126
questions.answer: The value wraps around due to overflow, producing -126

options: A compilation error because a + b is promoted to int
questions.answer: A compilation error because a + b is promoted to int

questions.question: Which of the following correctly stores the result of adding two byte values?
What is the result of 10 / 3 in Java?
What is the result of 10 / 3.0 in Java?
Given: int a = 5; double b = 2.0; What type is the result of a + b?
What does Java do when byte, short, or char values are used in an arithmetic expression?
options: Keeps them as their original type

questions.question: What is the result of casting (char) 65 in Java?
What is the difference between truncation and rounding when converting double to int?
options: Truncation removes the decimal portion; rounding adjusts to the nearest integer
Rounding removes the decimal portion; truncation adjusts to the nearest integer
questions.answer: Truncation removes the decimal portion; rounding adjusts to the nearest integer

questions.question: What does (int) 89.9 evaluate to in Java?
How do you perform proper rounding of a double value in Java instead of truncation?
What is the type of the expression: char letter = 'A'; letter + 1;?
How do you correctly get the character 'B' from char letter = 'A' using arithmetic?
options: char next = letter++;  // This always returns 'B'

questions.question: Given: byte a = 10; short b = 20; long c = 30L; What type is a + b + c?
Which of the following assignments will cause a compilation error?
Why does the formula (F - 32) * 5 / 9 produce incorrect results for Fahrenheit-to-Celsius conversion when F is an int?
options: Integer division truncates the result of 5 / 9 or the intermediate division
The formula itself is mathematically wrong
questions.answer: Integer division truncates the result of 5 / 9 or the intermediate division

questions.question: Which of the following is a widening conversion that can still lose information?
What happens when you try to assign a double value directly to a float variable without a cast?
options: The value is rounded to fit
A compilation error occurs due to possible loss of precision
questions.answer: A compilation error occurs due to possible loss of precision

questions.question: What is the result of casting (long) 3_000_000_000L to int?
options: A negative number due to overflow
questions.answer: A negative number due to overflow

questions.question: Which two approaches correctly produce 3.5 from dividing int p = 7 by int q = 2?
